scenes.title: The Bribe
scenes.description: A failing student discreetly offers money to bump their grade.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor... I can't fail. Maybe we can... work something out?"
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "A discreet envelope, left on the desk. What do you do?"

choices.label: ACCEPT
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Thank you, Professor. You won't regret this."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "...Fine. Your grade is now a C. Not a word to anyone."
choices.outcome: You accepted the bribe. Academic integrity has left the chat.

choices.label: KICK OUT
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Absolutely not. Get out of my office."
choices.outcome: You kicked the student out. The grade stays on the record.

scenes.title: Scholarship on the Line
scenes.description: A C-grade student begs for help before losing their scholarship.
scenes.banner: Grade: {grade} — A student wants to talk

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, my scholarship depends on a B. Can we talk?"
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "They look genuinely stressed. How do you respond?"

choices.label: EXTRA CREDIT
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Thank you! I won't let you down!"
choices.professorReply: Professor: "One bonus question next week. Don't waste it."
choices.outcome: You offered extra credit. Hope they earn it.

choices.label: STAND FIRM
choices.professorReply: Professor: "The grade stands. Work harder next term."
choices.outcome: You stood firm. The grade stays a C.

scenes.title: Thank-You Gift
scenes.description: An A student brings a small gift after an excellent exam.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, thank you! I brought you something small."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "A wrapped gift on the desk. Accept it?"

choices.label: ACCEPT GIFT
choices.professorReply: Professor: "That's very kind. You made my day."
choices.outcome: You accepted the gift graciously. Teaching feels worth it.

choices.label: POLITELY DECLINE
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Thank you, but I can't accept gifts from students."
choices.outcome: You declined professionally. Boundaries intact.

scenes.title: The Regrade Request
scenes.description: A B-grade student insists one question was graded too harshly.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, question three was basically correct. Can you regrade it?"
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "They've marked up the exam with notes. Regrade or hold the line?"

choices.label: REGRADE
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Thank you! I knew you'd be fair."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Fine. I'll review it tonight and update the score."
choices.outcome: You agreed to a regrade. Word may spread that your grading is negotiable.

choices.label: HOLD GRADE
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Alright. I still disagree, but thanks for listening."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I reviewed it again. The grade stands."
choices.outcome: You held firm. Consistency preserved.

scenes.title: The Parent Email
scenes.description: A failing student's parent emails demanding a grade change.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "My parent emailed the department about my grade... They're waiting for your reply."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "Pressure from home. Bend or document everything?"

choices.label: BUMP GRADE
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I'll move it to a D. This is a one-time exception."
choices.outcome: You changed the grade under pressure. Your inbox is quiet—for now.

choices.label: REFUSE & LOG
choices.professorReply: Professor: "The grade reflects the exam. I've documented my reasoning."
choices.outcome: You refused and documented. Professional, but expect follow-up emails.

scenes.title: The Cheating Tip
scenes.description: A student reports that someone copied answers during the exam.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, the person next to me was copying. I think you should know."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "An anonymous-style tip at your desk. Investigate or let it go?"

choices.label: INVESTIGATE
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I'll review the seating chart and camera footage."
choices.outcome: You opened an integrity review. The department notices your vigilance.

choices.label: DROP IT
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I don't have enough evidence. Let's move on."
choices.outcome: You let it slide. Faster day, murkier conscience.

scenes.title: The Extension Plea
scenes.description: A student begs for more time, claiming a family emergency.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, I had a family emergency. Can I retake tomorrow?"
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "Hard to verify, easy to sympathize. Grant an extension?"

choices.label: GRANT EXTENSION
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Thank you so much. I won't forget this."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "One retake tomorrow. Same format, no notes."
choices.outcome: You granted mercy. Compassion noted—maybe exploited later.

choices.label: NO EXTENSION
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "I guess I'll accept the grade then."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Policy is clear. The exam date was announced weeks ago."
choices.outcome: You enforced the deadline. Rules are rules.

scenes.title: The RA Offer
scenes.description: A high-performing student offers free research help—for a price in grades.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "I'd love to be your research assistant... if we can 'align' my grade first."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "Talent on display, ethics in question."

choices.label: HIRE & BUMP
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Fine. A on the exam, and you help with data entry."
choices.outcome: You traded grades for labor. Productive—and problematic.

choices.label: HIRE FAIRLY
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Fair enough. I still want the position."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Grade stays. Apply through the official RA posting."
choices.outcome: You separated merit from negotiation. Integrity intact.

scenes.title: The Dean Threat
scenes.description: An F-grade student threatens a formal complaint to the dean.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Fail me and I'm filing a complaint with the dean."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "Bluster or real threat? Stand your ground or compromise?"

choices.label: COMPROMISE
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I'll change it to a D. No complaint, no discussion."
choices.outcome: You bought peace. The dean never calls—but you know why.

choices.label: STAND GROUND
choices.professorReply: Professor: "File whatever you want. The exam speaks for itself."
choices.outcome: You held the line. Stressful, but your record is clean.

scenes.title: The Exam Leak
scenes.description: A student offers to share 'study materials' that look suspiciously like your exam.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, I found these 'study notes' online... they look like your exam."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "A folder of leaked questions. Buy silence or blow the whistle?"

choices.label: REPORT LEAK
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Thank you. I'm reporting this to integrity office immediately."
choices.outcome: You reported the leak. New exam needed, but justice served.

choices.label: IGNORE IT
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Really? You're just going to let it slide?"
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Can't prove anything. Let's pretend this never happened."
choices.outcome: You looked away. Easier day, compromised exam.

scenes.title: The Coffee Deal
scenes.description: A solid student offers daily coffee runs in exchange for 'flexible' grading.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "I run the campus café. Free lattes all semester—for a little grading flexibility?"
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "A gift card and a wink. Tempting on a Monday morning."

choices.label: ACCEPT DEAL
choices.professorReply: Professor: "...One latte. And we're never discussing this again."
choices.outcome: You traded ethics for caffeine. Delicious, dubious.

choices.label: DECLINE
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I pay for my own coffee. Grade stands."
choices.outcome: You declined. Bitter coffee, clean conscience.

scenes.title: The Impostor
scenes.description: A student claims someone else sat their exam and got a better grade.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "Professor, my twin took my seat by mistake. That's not my handwriting!"
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "A wild story with no proof. Believe them or verify?"

choices.label: VERIFY ID
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "Check the sign-in sheet. I wasn't even there!"
choices.professorReply: Professor: "I'll pull the seating log and ID photos."
choices.outcome: You investigated properly. Truth will out.

choices.label: DISMISS
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "You're ruining my life over a mix-up!"
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Nice try. The grade stays."
choices.outcome: You dismissed the claim. Quick resolution—maybe wrong.

scenes.title: The Donation Hint
scenes.description: A wealthy student's family hints at a building donation—if grades improve.

dialogue.studentOffer: {studentName}{majorSuffix}: "My family is discussing a library donation... they'd love to see a stronger transcript."
dialogue.professorPrompt: Professor: "A donation brochure on your desk. Ethics vs. renovations."

choices.label: ADJUST GRADE
choices.studentReply: {studentName}: "The new wing will have your name on a plaque."
choices.professorReply: Professor: "B minus. Don't make me regret this."
choices.outcome: You traded grades for institutional funding. The dean smiles.

choices.label: REFUSE
choices.professorReply: Professor: "Donations don't buy grades. Good day."
choices.outcome: You refused. The library renovation can wait.
